key_risks: Semiconductor/AI factor reversal if rates back up further given 10Y at 4.56% and sticky core PCE 3.2%
Taiwan/Korea geopolitical escalation given referenced cross-strait tensions could hit EWT and SMH simultaneously
Oil spike from Hormuz disruption could lift CPI further and pressure growth/tech multiples without benefiting our energy-light book
Consumer sentiment crash to 44.8 may presage recessionary surprise hurting cyclical semis and defense supply chains
Gold pullback if JPM's revised lower forecast ($5,243 vs prior $5,708) signals near-term consolidation after strong run

portfolio.rationale: Nvidia Q1 FY27 data center revenue +85% YoY confirms AI capex momentum; SMH strong trend (+13.8% 30d, +70% 6m) with Taiwan/Korea supply chain validation.
$1.5T FY27 defense budget request plus Iran/Hormuz geopolitical tensions provide strong near-term tailwind; ITA showing positive momentum.
Q1 GDP +13.7%, exports +35.3% YoY, semiconductor supply chain leverage; strongest 7d return (+6.09%) signals continuation.
Hedge against sticky inflation (CPI 3.8%, PCE 3.5%), geopolitical risk (Hormuz), consumer sentiment collapse to 44.8, and high CAPE 41.6 equity valuations.
Diversified momentum exposure capturing the prevailing trend regime (+8.08% 30d) while reducing single-theme concentration risk.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward the strongest persistent trends (AI/semis, defense, Taiwan tech supply chain) with gold as a hedge against stagflationary signals (CPI 3.8%, PCE 3.5%, sentiment collapse to 44.8, CAPE 41.6) and Hormuz/oil risk. Momentum factor provides diversified trend exposure to complement thematic concentration.
rationale_summary: Concentrate in AI/semiconductor supply chain (SMH, Taiwan) and defense (ITA) which have demonstrated earnings-backed momentum, balanced by gold hedge against re-accelerating inflation, geopolitical risk, and elevated equity valuations.
